_comment: Canonical model-to-role assignment. The source for install.sh (adapterType/adapterConfig.model at hire). Principle 1: the builder side and the adversary side are MANDATORILY on different providers.

claude.note: builder side. The model is set by the claude CLI config; by default the current Claude. Set CLAUDE_CLI if the binary is not in PATH (default: claude). Env vars carry repo/deploy paths so prompt placeholders resolve at runtime.

codex.note: adversary side. A DIFFERENT provider from Claude → Principle 1. Use SPARINGLY (subscription quota, may hit limits) — only on the critical path (first_class/complex_irreversible). Lower the reasoning effort to save: -c model_reasoning_effort=low|medium.

process.note: The Objective Verifier and tooling agents — WITHOUT a model, the verdict comes from a real run.

kimi.note: adversary side. kimi K2.7 via OpenCode (opencode-go/kimi-k2.7-code) + opencode_local adapter — a FULL agentic critic on a different model family (decorrelation, Principle 1). Replaced acpx_local (broke on kimi 0.18: ACP session returns backend model no-access) and the process+kimi -p fallback (text-only, could not act as a review agent). OpenCode must be installed + authenticated; use the opencode-go provider (NOT zen).

opencode.status: requires opencode CLI and configured providers
opencode.note: builder side via OpenCode. Set OPENCODE_BUILDER_MODEL to a provider/model slug (e.g. anthropic/claude-opus-4-7). The installer can auto-detect the model from ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json. OpenCode must be installed and authenticated (`opencode --version`).

opencode-critic.status: requires opencode CLI and a second model family
opencode-critic.note: adversary side via OpenCode on a DIFFERENT backend model family (e.g. openai/gpt-5.2-pro vs builder anthropic/claude-opus-4-7). Set OPENCODE_CRITIC_MODEL; the installer defaults to openai/gpt-5.2-pro. This is weaker than a fully separate provider (Kimi) but still provides model-family decorrelation.

roleAssignment._principle1_check: builder/* and synthesis/* on claude(Opus); critics adversary/steelman/rubric on kimi(K2.7) — DIFFERENT providers → decorrelation Principle 1 RESTORED (P0 closed 2026-06-16). For opencode mode the installer overrides this via ROLE_PROVIDER_MAP.

_decorrelation: Critics adversary/steelman-critic/rubric-auditor on the SECOND provider kimi (K2.7 Code via acpx_local, see the kimi provider + docs/kimi-adapter-setup.md), builder/synthesis on claude(Opus). Different model families → Principle 1 (decorrelation) holds. Adversary confirmed E2E. The second provider is swappable (kimi / codex / opencode-critic / another with a different family).
todo: Operator's DECISION (2026-06-14): do NOT downgrade models. Opus is both smarter and faster than Sonnet/Haiku per the Operator's data. All claude roles — on Opus (the claude_local default). Speedup is STRUCTURAL (route-depth calibration, stage parallelism, terse front-gate prompts), NOT a model swap. The spec's note about a 'cheap fast model for the classifier' is overruled by this decision. Do not use OpenRouter.
Lower the codex reasoning effort for the adversary roles (quota economy).
